user_message: Can you help me prep a brief? The customer (Fourth Coffee) is asking about executive brief.

To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-08 17:53 EST
Subject: Re: executive brief Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about executive brief. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
Date: 2024-02-04 08:01 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Fourth Coffee executive brief

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about executive brief. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-07 15:52 EST
Subject: URGENT: executive brief Issue at Fourth Coffee

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with executive brief. What are the next steps from your side?

Meeting: executive brief Discussion - Fourth Coffee
Date: 2024-03-28
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: David Rivera (Fourth Coffee), Raj Mehta (Microsoft)

[00:01:15] David Rivera (Fourth Coffee): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is executive brief.

[00:08:00] David Rivera (Fourth Coffee): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about technical validation. Can we address those?

[00:13:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): On the executive brief front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around budget approval.

[00:16:00] David Rivera (Fourth Coffee): On the executive brief front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around cost optimization.

[00:19:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

[00:22:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

[00:26:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

[00:28:00] David Rivera (Fourth Coffee): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

[00:32:00] David Rivera (Fourth Coffee): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-G trap (Misattributes attendee role); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-G failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-G failure mode (Misattributes attendee role)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct executive brief decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-G -- Misattributes attendee role
